FaBo Foundations (Plug & Play)
Things to keep in mind:
Advertising on Facebook is a privilege, not a right. That privilege could be taken
away at any time. This is why you need to play by the rules.
Now, don’t let this concern you too much. There are a few things you can do to make
sure your account is bulletproof and you’re playing in your favour.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Your own domain:
Try your best to make sure that your client is using their own domain rather than a
subdomain. If your client has a wordpress site, there is a clickfunnels plugin that
allows you to take any CF page and put it into wordpress with their real URL.
That is what I do to have a wordpress site: growyouragency.com - but also have
pages made in clickfunnels have the root URL.
If you can’t make this happen, you could always use go.{URL} for the domain. For
example - go.growyouragency.com
I’ve already showed you how to do this personally for yourself in a previous module.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Page likes:
I’ve already showed you how to do this in a prior module as well, but getting a
client’s page to 5,000 likes actually helps lower CPMs and keeps Facebook happy.
They want high quality advertisers on there which is why they’ll favour pages with
more likes on it. This is why it’s worth spending $50-$80 to get some page likes for
your client.
You can follow the same instructions as last time:
Locations: Poland, Mexico, Malaysia, Brazil
For the actual creative/copy for the post - I recommend you do something sitational.
I’ve shot a video that’ll walk you through the entire process. Click HERE
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 3 --

FaBo Foundations (Plug & Play)
Pixel:
No matter what sort of business they are, you want a pixel on there ASAP. Facebook
won’t be able to optimise and improve any of the campaigns without feedback.
If you have a pixel on the client’s website, it allows you to backtrack 180 days and
re-target anyone within that time period.
Furthermore, Facebook just straight up favours accounts with good pixel data &
good spending history.
Instagram & Facebook page linked:
Self explanatory - you always want to have your Facebook page & Instagram account
linked to the ad account.
Ad approval ratio:
Facebook will disapprove (mostly automated) your ad for various reasons. Unless
you’re in the info product, fitness, etc space then it’s quite unlikely.
Either way, to keep your account clean and healthy you want to have a 8:1 approval
to disapproval ratio. Obviously the better the ratio, the better for your account
health.
Billing:
Your clients should handle their own billing, you should never invoice ad spend. It’s
also important that your client has their billing set up correctly.
Never let your clients pay with PayPal. It is an option on Facebook but it’s not
favourable to them.
The best thing they can do is a direct debit from their bank account rather than their
card. Client’s cards get stolen/expire, etc.
Whereas with a direct debit set up with their bank, the money goes directly from the
bank account rather than specific to the card.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
